bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility (0.20). I will show a slight willingness to compromise by conceding marginally on my least important issue (issueD) while maintaining my most preferred values for all other issues, aiming for an initial high utility of 0.973.

The opponent has made significant concessions on my highest-priority issue (issueA: valueA), suggesting they may not assign it high weight. Since time is only t=0.04, I should retain valueA for issueA and valueA for my second highest priority, issueB. I will moderately concede on issueC (to valueB) and issueD (to valueC) to encourage further cooperation while maintaining high utility (0.893) for myself.

The current time is very early (t=0.06), but the opponent has shown a willingness to compromise on issue A and has been varying their requests on issues B and D. Since issue A is my highest priority (utility weight 0.4), I will firmly hold onto value A. To reciprocate their moves and find early acceptable trade-offs, I will meet them on issue B by offering value B and keep issue D at value C, while reverting issue C back to value A to maintain an overall high utility (0.86) for this stage of the negotiation.

The opponent just made a significant concession on my highest-weighted issues, offering valueA for both issueA and issueB. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.08), I want to lock in this mutual agreement on the top issues while making a concession on the remaining ones. I will offer valueD for my least important issue (issueD) to match their preference, while keeping issueC at valueB to maintain a high overall utility of 0.866.

The opponent is making generous compromises early on, offering combinations like 'issueA: valueA' and 'issueB: valueB' which yield high utility for me. At time t=0.1, the negotiation is just beginning, so I am going to maintain a high target utility (around 0.89) by holding firm on the most important issues (issueA and issueB to 'valueA'). Since the opponent seems to value 'issueD: valueD', I'll offer a minor concession on issueD while integrating their previous 'issueC: valueB' offer.

The opponent is offering values that give me maximum utility on my most important issues (issueA and issueB). This brings their latest offer's utility to a very strong 0.814. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), I will secure these gains and counter by asking for a slight improvement on issueC (requesting valueB, which they were previously willing to offer at t=0.10), while conceding on issueD to match their preference (valueD). This yields a utility of 0.866 for me while accommodating their latest moves.

The current time fraction is very early (t=0.14), so I want to maintain a relatively high utility above 0.80. The opponent has recently shown a willingness to accept `issueA: valueA` and occasionally `issueB: valueA`, which are my two highest priority issues. To explore a fair tradeoff, I will offer a combination that retains my top priorities for issues A and B while conceding on issues C and D toward values the opponent has previously proposed.

The opponent's latest offer provides me with my top choices for my two most important issues (issueA and issueB), my second choice for issueC, and my last choice for issueD, which is my lowest priority issue. This yields a total utility of 0.866, well above my minimum threshold. To secure this excellent outcome early on, I will accept their proposal by returning their exact bid.
